name: Anterior STEMI
description: 58yo male with classic anterior ST-elevation myocardial infarction

symptoms.text: 58-year-old male with sudden onset severe substernal chest pain radiating to left arm for 45 minutes. Diaphoresis, nausea, and shortness of breath. History of hypertension, diabetes mellitus type 2, and smoking (30 pack-years). Family history of MI in father at age 52.
symptoms.chief_complaint: Severe substernal chest pain radiating to left arm

ecg.st_segment: ST-segment elevation in V1-V4 with reciprocal depression in II, III, aVF
ecg.interpretation: Acute anterior STEMI with reciprocal changes
